{{Quote|No! Mesa People are bein destroyen!|Boss Gallo, during the destruction of Otoh Sancture|<ref name="SW:GB" />}}
'''Otoh Sancture''' was a wealthy [[Gungan/Legends|Gungan]] [[City/Legends|settlement]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]] ruled by [[Boss/Legends|Boss]] [[Gallo/Legends|Gallo]] and located west of [[Lake Paonga/Legends|Lake Paonga]]. It was [[Destruction of Otoh Sancture|destroyed]] by [[Diet/Legends|carnivorous]] [[Bursa/Legends|bursas]] under the command of the [[Warlord/Legends|warlord]] Boss [[Rogoe/Legends|Rogoe]] in the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[3000 BBY]] while Gallo was out [[Hunting/Legends|hunting]] [[Nerf/Legends|nerfs]] for the annual [[Sacred Feast]]. Gallo later rebuilt the town and used it to recruit a small [[Army/Legends|army]] to [[Battle of Otoh Sancture|defeat]] the bursas.

==Description==
Otoh Sancture was a [[Gungan/Legends|Gungan]] [[City/Legends|stronghold]]<ref name="CSWE">{{CSWECite|II|429|Otoh Sancture}}</ref> on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]]. It was located in the [[Gungan Swamp]] on a peninsula just west of [[Lake Paonga/Legends|Lake Paonga]] and was surrounded on three sides by cliffs and a [[Water|river]], and on the fourth side by a [[Tree/Legends|forest]]. The city contained thirty-one [[prefab shelter]]s, a [[command center]], seven [[Sentry tower/Legends|sentry towers]], a [[food processing center]], a [[carbon processing center]], an [[animal nursery]], and four [[Farm/Legends|farms]] before it was [[Destruction of Otoh Sancture|destroyed]] in [[3000 BBY]]. Otoh Sancture's original fauna included several [[Sentience/Legends|non-sentient]] [[glurrg]] workers that were killed during the city's fall.<ref name="SW:GB" />

==History==
[[File:SantctureMassacre.jpg|thumb|left|300px|The destruction of Otoh Sancture]]
During a [[War of the Gungan tribes|war]] between the numerous Gungan tribes in the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] 3000 BBY,<ref name="EA" /> Otoh Sancture remained neutral and under the control of the wealthy [[Boss/Legends|Boss]] [[Gallo/Legends|Gallo]]. As a result, Otoh Sancture became a hub for a large amount of [[Merchant/Legends|trade]], commerce, gossip, and [[Spy/Legends|spying]]. Its location west of Lake Paonga made it a way-station and go-between for all the local tribes, which resulted in a large amount of revenue for the city and its inhabitants. Sometime during the [[Ground warfare/Legends|war]] between the tribes, the city celebrated the annual [[Sacred Feast]], a tradition that required the Boss of each city to [[Hunting/Legends|hunt]] and gather enough animals to feed the entire population of the village. While Gallo was out hunting [[Nerf/Legends|nerfs]] for the Feast, he heard a [[Unidentified Gungan villager (Otoh Sancture)|villager's]] cry for help as a clan of [[Bursa/Legends|bursas]], large semi-sentient [[Diet/Legends|carnivores]], had attacked and destroyed Otoh Sancture, [[Death/Legends|killing]] all the residents and glurrg workers. Gallo mourned greatly for the loss of his people.<ref name="SW:GB" />

After roaming in the forest for a short amount of [[Time/Legends|time]], Gallo was told by a [[Militiagung/Legends|militiagung]] from an enemy [[Rogoe's army|militia]] that the [[Warlord/Legends|warlord]] Boss [[Rogoe/Legends|Rogoe]] was responsible for the death of Gallo's people. In honor of those killed by the bursas, Gallo eventually rebuilt the city in a new area just east of the old location and abundant in [[Nova crystal/Legends|nova crystals]], [[Ore/Legends|ore]], [[Foodstuff/Legends|food]], and trees—which could be used as a source of [[Carbon/Legends|carbon]]—as all four were necessary resources. He used the new settlement to recruit a small [[Army/Legends|force]], with which Gallo [[Battle of Otoh Sancture|attacked]] and defeated the bursa clan that had since made its nests in the ruins of the old city. After the victory at Otoh Sancture, Gallo went on to unite the Gungan tribes, forming the [[Gungan Grand Army/Legends|Gungan Grand Army]], which he used to [[Raid on Spearhead|defeat]] Rogoe.<ref name="SW:GB" />

==Behind the scenes==
Otoh Sancture appears in the [[2001]] [[Wikipedia:Personal computer|PC]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds]]''<ref name="SW:GB" /> and its [[2002]] expansion pack, ''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds: Clone Campaigns]]''.<ref name="SW:GB:CC">''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds: Clone Campaigns]]''</ref> The city is destroyed during the game's first Gungan campaign mission, "Otoh Sancture," during a scripted event. When the city is rebuilt, it is left up to the player as to how it is built and what buildings are included.<ref name="SW:GB" /> The city also received an entry in ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'', published in [[2008]], where it is described as an underwater stronghold, in contrast to the terrestrial city seen in the video game.<ref name="CSWE" />
